old_claim.note: Galleon ship design was treated as an enabling capability for naval logbooks without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct galleon-design dependency remains because shipboard logbooks can be kept on multiple vessel types and are scoped to records, not to one hull form.

source_shape.source_locator: Nautical logbook overview: the article treats logbooks as ship records of operation and navigation, without limiting them to galleons.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports naval logbooks as a general shipboard record practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Galleon design is a vessel-specific context; keeping it direct would incorrectly narrow the record practice to one ship design family.

ontology_before: Naval Logbooks depended directly on Galleon Ship Design, implying galleons were required for the logbook practice.
ontology_after: Naval Logbooks keeps Paper Making as the direct prerequisite and removes the vessel-design edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: galleon_ship_design is no longer a direct prerequisite for naval_logbooks.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to logbooks specifically aboard galleons.
A stronger source showed galleon design directly enabled the scoped logbook practice.
A separate galleon_navigation_records node were introduced and intentionally tied to galleon hulls.
short_reason: Galleon design is vessel context, not the logbook prerequisite.
